description.p: Quill is a privacy-first dictation app. Hold a hotkey, speak, release — the transcript appears wherever your cursor is. Runs Whisper locally (private, offline) or via Groq Cloud (fast, low cost).
Quill es una app de dictado privacy-first. Mantenés un atajo, hablás, soltás — el texto aparece donde esté tu cursor. Corre Whisper localmente (privado, offline) o via Groq Cloud (rápido, barato).

ul.li: Local engine with downloadable Whisper models (tiny → large-v3)
Groq Cloud engine for sub-second latency
Transcription-quality features: Silero VAD pre-processing, custom prompt presets, vocabulary post-substitution
Searchable history of every transcription stored locally

screenshot.caption: Recording overlay shown over a focused application

description.p: Linux compatibility round-out — Flatpak / Flathub manifest, wizard adaptive setup card for Wayland compositors without GlobalShortcuts portal (GNOME <48, KDE Plasma 5, Sway, Hyprland, wlroots), and hybrid pill + native desktop notifications for ClipboardOnly / Error states on Wayland.

Transcription quality pack — Silero VAD pre-processing, vocabulary post-substitution, custom prompt presets (General, Código, Email, Casual + custom), polished UI from the Claude Design v0.3.0 bundle.
Wayland round-out — Spanish pill labels on all platforms, state-aware tray tooltip, persistent RemoteDesktop restore token (no more consent dialog every launch).
Linux bug-fix release — wizard wizard_version persistence, wl-clipboard-rs for Wayland clipboard, libei auto-paste via XDG RemoteDesktop, dual hotkey backend (portal + evdev fallback).
Linux support lands. X11 + Wayland sessions, .deb / .AppImage / .rpm installers alongside Windows NSIS + MSI.
First public release. Windows-only, push-to-talk dictation with local + Groq Cloud engines.
